title: najas guadalupensis subsp. olivacea
intro: Najas guadalupensis subsp. olivacea, also known as Guadalupe water wort, is a submerged aquatic plant species belonging to the Najadaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, Najas guadalupensis subsp. olivacea can be made into a tea or infused in water to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
